{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345}}|children={{ill|Emperor Itoku|qid=Q271536}}; {{ill|Okimi no Mikoto|qid=Q11492323}}; {{ill|Shikitsuhiko no Mikoto|qid=Q106617602}}|father={{ill|Hae|qid=Q85883497}}; {{ill|Kamo no Kimi|qid=Q55809532}}|mother={{ill|Q97478962|qid=Q97478962}}|native_name=渟名底仲媛}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11561868]]
Wikidata name: Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan, and Empress Dowager.}}

{{Infobox royalty
| name         = Nunasokonakatsu-hime
| succession   = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]]
| reign        = 546–510 BC
(36 years)
| predecessor  = [[Isuzuyori-hime]]
| successor    = [[Amonotoyototsu-hime]]
| spouse       = [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]]
| issue        = [[Tokotsuhikoirone]]
[[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]], [[Ikisomimi no mikoto]] [[Shikitsuhiko]]
| father       = [[Kamo no Okimi]]
| mother       = [[Mirahime]]
| succession1  = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]]
| reign1       = 510 BC-?
| predecessor1 = Isuzuyori-hime
| successor1   = Amonotoyototsu-hime
}}

'''Nunasokonakatsu-hime''' (渟名底仲媛命) was Empress Consort from 546-510BC<ref>Anston, p., 41 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref name=":0">and Anston, p. 1.42 (Vol. 1)</ref> and then Empress Dowager from 510BC to an unknown date. <ref name=":0" />
== Life ==
There exact number of children differs between the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]]. In the Kojiki they have 3, Tokotsuhikoirone, Emperor Itoku, and Shikitsuhiko.<ref name=":1">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref> Yet in the Nihon shoki they have another, Ikisomimi.<ref name=":2">W. G. ASTON, C.M.G. (1896) “Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref> only the Kojiki states her father as Kamo no Okimi. <ref name=":1" /><ref>Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.</ref> The Empress's life isn't recorded in the Kojiki or Nihon shoki much other than her family and titles. <ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2024-03-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nunasokonakatsu-hime]][[fa:نوناسوکوناکاتسو هیمه]][[ja:渟名底仲媛]][[ko:누나소코나카쓰히메]][[zh:渟名底仲媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Annei|WD=Q313345}}|children={{ill|Emperor Itoku|WD=Q271536}}; {{ill|Okimi no Mikoto|WD=Q11492323}}; {{ill|Shikitsuhiko no Mikoto|WD=Q106617602}}|father={{ill|Hae|WD=Q85883497}}; {{ill|Kamo no Kimi|WD=Q55809532}}|mother={{ill|Q97478962|WD=Q97478962}}|native_name=渟名底仲媛}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11561868]]
Wikidata name: Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Empress Consort of Japan, and Empress Dowager.}}

{{Infobox royalty
| name         = Nunasokonakatsu-hime
| succession   = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]]
| reign        = 546–510 BC
(36 years)
| predecessor  = [[Isuzuyori-hime]]
| successor    = [[Amonotoyototsu-hime]]
| spouse       = [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]]
| issue        = [[Tokotsuhikoirone]]
[[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]], [[Ikisomimi no mikoto]] [[Shikitsuhiko]]
| father       = [[Kamo no Okimi]]
| mother       = [[Mirahime]]
| succession1  = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]]
| reign1       = 510 BC-?
| predecessor1 = Isuzuyori-hime
| successor1   = Amonotoyototsu-hime
}}

'''Nunasokonakatsu-hime''' (渟名底仲媛命) was Empress Consort from 546-510BC<ref>Anston, p., 41 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref name=":0">and Anston, p. 1.42 (Vol. 1)</ref> and then Empress Dowager from 510BC to an unknown date. <ref name=":0" />
== Life ==
There exact number of children differs between the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]]. In the Kojiki they have 3, Tokotsuhikoirone, Emperor Itoku, and Shikitsuhiko.<ref name=":1">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref> Yet in the Nihon shoki they have another, Ikisomimi.<ref name=":2">W. G. ASTON, C.M.G. (1896) “Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref> only the Kojiki states her father as Kamo no Okimi. <ref name=":1" /><ref>Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.</ref> The Empress's life isn't recorded in the Kojiki or Nihon shoki much other than her family and titles. <ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2024-03-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

{{Infobox royalty
| name         = Nunasokonakatsu-hime
| succession   = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]]
| reign        = 546–510 BC
(36 years)
| predecessor  = [[Isuzuyori-hime]]
| successor    = [[Amonotoyototsu-hime]]
| spouse       = [[:en:Emperor Annei|Emperor Annei]]
| issue        = [[Tokotsuhikoirone]]
[[:en:Emperor Itoku|Emperor Itoku]], [[Ikisomimi no mikoto]] [[Shikitsuhiko]]
| father       = [[Kamo no Okimi]]
| mother       = [[Mirahime]]
| succession1  = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]]
| reign1       = 510 BC-?
| predecessor1 = Isuzuyori-hime
| successor1   = Amonotoyototsu-hime
}}

'''Nunasokonakatsu-hime''' (渟名底仲媛命) was Empress Consort from 546-510BC<ref>Anston, p., 41 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref name=":0">and Anston, p. 1.42 (Vol. 1)</ref> and then Empress Dowager from 510BC to an unknown date. <ref name=":0" />
== Life ==
There exact number of children differs between the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]]. In the Kojiki they have 3, Tokotsuhikoirone, Emperor Itoku, and Shikitsuhiko.<ref name=":1">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref> Yet in the Nihon shoki they have another, Ikisomimi.<ref name=":2">W. G. ASTON, C.M.G. (1896) “Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref> only the Kojiki states her father as Kamo no Okimi. <ref name=":1" /><ref>Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.</ref> The Empress's life isn't recorded in the Kojiki or Nihon shoki much other than her family and titles. <ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2024-03-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

'''Nunasokonakatsu-hime''' (渟名底仲媛命) was Empress Consort from 546-510BC<ref>Anston, p., 41 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref name=":0">and Anston, p. 1.42 (Vol. 1)</ref> and then Empress Dowager from 510BC to an unknown date. <ref name=":0" />
== Life ==
There exact number of children differs between the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]]. In the Kojiki they have 3, Tokotsuhikoirone, Emperor Itoku, and Shikitsuhiko.<ref name=":1">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref> Yet in the Nihon shoki they have another, Ikisomimi.<ref name=":2">W. G. ASTON, C.M.G. (1896) “Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref> only the Kojiki states her father as Kamo no Okimi. <ref name=":1" /><ref>Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.</ref> The Empress's life isn't recorded in the Kojiki or Nihon shoki much other than her family and titles. <ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2024-03-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

'''Nunasokonakatsu-hime''' (渟名底仲媛命) was Empress Consort from 546-510BC<ref>Anston, p., 41 (Vol. 1)</ref><ref name=":0">and Anston, p. 1.42 (Vol. 1)</ref> and then Empress Dowager from 510BC to an unknown date. <ref name=":0" />
== Life ==
There exact number of children differs between the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]]. In the Kojiki they have 3, Tokotsuhikoirone, Emperor Itoku, and Shikitsuhiko.<ref name=":1">Yasumaro. O, translated by Gustav Heldt. (2014) “Kojiki. An Account of Ancient Matters”. New York: Columbia University Press.</ref> Yet in the Nihon shoki they have another, Ikisomimi.<ref name=":2">W. G. ASTON, C.M.G. (1896) “Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697”. Tuttle Publishing.</ref> only the Kojiki states her father as Kamo no Okimi. <ref name=":1" /><ref>Ponsonby, F. (1959) “The Imperial House of Japan.” Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society.</ref> The Empress's life isn't recorded in the Kojiki or Nihon shoki much other than her family and titles. <ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2024-03-21 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>
